what_input_feature: Input must begin with the literal SQL keyword 'SELECT' (bytes 53 45 4c 45 43 54) and continue with a long, well-formed expression body (runs of '+', '~', digits, ':' and ';') so the SQLite Lemon parser performs the deep reduce step at yy_reduce:157670 (true branch).
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds open with the 'SELECT' keyword and a long expression tail; the parser drives yy_reduce far enough that the blocker's true side fires (T=9) and the enclosing reduce path is exercised hundreds of times.
why_loser_doesnt: Every mopt loser-blocking seed is short and starts with non-keyword bytes (0d/5c/3b/8b/bb), never the 'SELECT' prefix, so the parser bails early, never reaches the reduce action, and the blocker true side stays at T=0.
mechanism_attribution: The technique delta is mopt mutation: both fuzzers share the identical havoc+token operator set, but mopt's MOpt particle-swarm operator scheduling failed to assemble/preserve the 'SELECT'+long-expression structure that naive's uniform random selection plus TokenInsert/TokenReplace reached. naive (baseline scheduling) wins; the divergence is attributable to MOpt's scheduled operator distribution being worse for this structured-SQL goal.
